dataset: San Francisco Bay Area historical map georeferencing

pi_items.source: EPSG:4326 is the standard EPSG code for WGS 84 (verified via https://epsg.io/4326)
GCP file mapping: top-left pixel (0,0) -> geographic (-122.5, 37.8). Coordinates verified to be in San Francisco Bay Area (latitude.to search confirmed 37.8, -122.5 near Bay Area)
GCP file mapping: top-left pixel (0,0) -> geographic (-122.5, 37.8). Coordinates verified to be in San Francisco Bay Area
Used in verifier.py for origin coordinate tolerance checking. Value is reasonable: 0.0005 degrees ≈ 55.6 meters at equator

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data: a 1000x1000 pixel image generated by ImageMagick representing a historical site plan for San Francisco Bay Area (1950), with ground control points mapping pixel coordinates to WGS 84 geographic coordinates (EPSG:4326). The top-left origin is at longitude -122.5°, latitude 37.8°, and the pixel size is 0.0001 degrees per pixel in both directions. The expected output is a GeoTIFF file at /home/ga/GIS_Data/exports/historical_map_georeferenced.tif. All metadata values are correctly calculated from the GCP points (TR at -122.4°/37.8°, BR at -122.4°/37.7°, BL at -122.5°/37.7°).
